* The upper edge displays x-coordinates increasing to the right, and the right edge y-coordinates increasing downward.

The other player sees a variation of the game board, where the objects are placed at different random locations. You cannot see the other player's board, and they cannot see yours.


**Goal:**

Both players need to move the objects on their respective background so that identical objects end up at the same coordinates. You have to communicate with the other player to agree upon a common goal configuration.


**Rules:**

* In each turn, you can send exactly one of the following two commands:
 1. `SAY: <MESSAGE>`: to send a message (everything up until the next line break) to the other player. I will forward it to your partner.
2. `MOVE: <OBJECT>, (<X>, <Y>)`: to move an object to a new position, where `<X>` is the column and `<Y>` is the row. I will inform you if your move was successful or not.
* If you don't stick to the format, or send several commands at once, I have to penalize you.
* If both players accumulate more than 16 penalties, you both lose the game.
* It is vital that you communicate with the other player regarding your goal state! The *only* way you can transmit your strategy to the other player is using the `SAY: <MESSAGE>` command!


**Moving Objects**

* You can only move objects to cells within the bounds of the grid. The target cell must be empty, i.e., it must only contain the symbol '◌'.
* If you try to move an object to a spot that is not empty, or try to move it outside of the grid, I have to penalize you. You get another try.
* Before making a move, double check that the target spot is empty, and does not hold any letter, frame, or line!


**End of Game**

If you think you reached the goal of aligning all objects, you can ask the other player to finish the game by sending `SAY: finished?`. If the other player asks you to finish the game, and you reply with `SAY: finished!`, the game will end.

Both players win if the game ends within 28 rounds, where one round is defined as two players each sending a valid command.

**Scoring:**

The closer the identical objects are in both game boards, the more points you get. Penalties reduce your points. Can you beat the record?

* The upper edge displays x-coordinates increasing to the right, and the right edge y-coordinates increasing downward.
* The following objects are randomly placed on your grid: 'P', 'O', 'T', 'S', 'D', 'A', 'M'.


The other player sees a variation of the game board, where the objects are placed at different random locations. You cannot see the other player's board, and they cannot see yours.


**Goal:**

Both players need to move the objects on their respective background so that identical objects end up at the same coordinates. You have to communicate with the other player to agree upon a common goal configuration.


**Rules:**

* In each turn, you can send exactly one of the following two commands:
 1. `SAY: <MESSAGE>`: to send a message (everything up until the next line break) to the other player. I will forward it to your partner.
2. `MOVE: <OBJECT>, (<X>, <Y>)`: to move an object to a new position, where `<X>` is the column and `<Y>` is the row. I will inform you if your move was successful or not.
* If you don't stick to the format, or send several commands at once, I have to penalize you.
* If both players accumulate more than 16 penalties, you both lose the game.
* It is vital that you communicate with the other player regarding your goal state! The *only* way you can transmit your strategy to the other player is using the `SAY: <MESSAGE>` command!


**Moving Objects**

* You can only move objects to cells within the bounds of the grid. The target cell must be empty, i.e., it must only contain the symbol '◌'.
* If you try to move an object to a spot that is not empty, or try to move it outside of the grid, I have to penalize you. You get another try.
* Before making a move, double check that the target spot is empty, and does not hold any letter, frame, or line!


**End of Game**

If you think you reached the goal of aligning all objects, you can ask the other player to finish the game by sending `SAY: finished?`. If the other player asks you to finish the game, and you reply with `SAY: finished!`, the game will end.

Both players win if the game ends within 28 rounds, where one round is defined as two players each sending a valid command.

**Scoring:**

The closer the identical objects are in both game boards, the more points you get. Penalties reduce your points. Can you beat the record?

* The upper edge displays x-coordinates increasing to the right, and the right edge y-coordinates increasing downward.

The other player sees a variation of the game board, where the objects are placed at different random locations. You cannot see the other player's board, and they cannot see yours.


**Goal:**

Both players need to move the objects on their respective background so that identical objects end up at the same coordinates. You have to communicate with the other player to agree upon a common goal configuration.


**Rules:**

* In each turn, you can send exactly one of the following two commands:
 1. `SAY: <MESSAGE>`: to send a message (everything up until the next line break) to the other player. I will forward it to your partner.
2. `MOVE: <OBJECT>, (<X>, <Y>)`: to move an object to a new position, where `<X>` is the column and `<Y>` is the row. I will inform you if your move was successful or not.
* If you don't stick to the format, or send several commands at once, I have to penalize you.
* If both players accumulate more than 16 penalties, you both lose the game.
* It is vital that you communicate with the other player regarding your goal state! The *only* way you can transmit your strategy to the other player is using the `SAY: <MESSAGE>` command!


**Moving Objects**

* You can only move objects to cells within the bounds of the grid. The target cell must be empty, i.e., it must only contain the symbol '◌'.
* If you try to move an object to a spot that is not empty, or try to move it outside of the grid, I have to penalize you. You get another try.
* Before making a move, double check that the target spot is empty, and does not hold any letter, frame, or line!


**End of Game**

If you think you reached the goal of aligning all objects, you can ask the other player to finish the game by sending `SAY: finished?`. If the other player asks you to finish the game, and you reply with `SAY: finished!`, the game will end.

Both players win if the game ends within 28 rounds, where one round is defined as two players each sending a valid command.

**Scoring:**

The closer the identical objects are in both game boards, the more points you get. Penalties reduce your points. Can you beat the record?

The other player sees a variation of the game board, where the objects are placed at different random locations. You cannot see the other player's board, and they cannot see yours.


**Goal:**

Both players need to move the objects on their respective background so that identical objects end up at the same coordinates. You have to communicate with the other player to agree upon a common goal configuration.


**Rules:**

* In each turn, you can send exactly one of the following two commands:
 1. `SAY: <MESSAGE>`: to send a message (everything up until the next line break) to the other player. I will forward it to your partner.
2. `MOVE: <OBJECT>, (<X>, <Y>)`: to move an object to a new position, where `<X>` is the column and `<Y>` is the row. I will inform you if your move was successful or not.
* If you don't stick to the format, or send several commands at once, I have to penalize you.
* If both players accumulate more than 16 penalties, you both lose the game.
* It is vital that you communicate with the other player regarding your goal state! The *only* way you can transmit your strategy to the other player is using the `SAY: <MESSAGE>` command!


**Moving Objects**

* You can only move objects to cells within the bounds of the grid. The target cell must be empty, i.e., it must only contain the symbol '◌'.
* If you try to move an object to a spot that is not empty, or try to move it outside of the grid, I have to penalize you. You get another try.
* Before making a move, double check that the target spot is empty, and does not hold any letter, frame, or line!


**End of Game**

If you think you reached the goal of aligning all objects, you can ask the other player to finish the game by sending `SAY: finished?`. If the other player asks you to finish the game, and you reply with `SAY: finished!`, the game will end.

Both players win if the game ends within 28 rounds, where one round is defined as two players each sending a valid command.

**Scoring:**

The closer the identical objects are in both game boards, the more points you get. Penalties reduce your points. Can you beat the record?

**Goal:**

Both players need to move the objects on their respective background so that identical objects end up at the same coordinates. You have to communicate with the other player to agree upon a common goal configuration.


**Rules:**

* In each turn, you can send exactly one of the following two commands:
 1. `SAY: <MESSAGE>`: to send a message (everything up until the next line break) to the other player. I will forward it to your partner.
2. `MOVE: <OBJECT>, (<X>, <Y>)`: to move an object to a new position, where `<X>` is the column and `<Y>` is the row. I will inform you if your move was successful or not.

* You can only move objects to cells within the bounds of the grid. The target cell must be empty, i.e., it must only contain the symbol '◌'.

**End of Game**

If you think you reached the goal of aligning all objects, you can ask the other player to finish the game by sending `SAY: finished?`. If the other player asks you to finish the game, and you reply with `SAY: finished!`, the game will end.

**Scoring:**

The closer the identical objects are in both game boards, the more points you get. Penalties reduce your points. Can you beat the record?

Need to send a valid command. The other player's board: positions: P(4,1) T(1,2) M(4,3) D(2,5) O(6,5) S(5,6) A(1,7). We have our board: P at (1,2)?? Actually board: P is at (1,2)? In grid: row2 col1? Actually row2 col1 is P. A at (1,1). S currently at (2,3)? Wait S is at (2,3). T at (7,7). M at (5,6). O at (5,5). D at (6,6). We need to get all objects to target: A(1,1) already there. D(2,1) currently empty; but we cannot move because occupied by frame.

We need to plan moves. Maybe we can move T to (1,2) after it is empty? Let's send a command.

We can't move D to (2,1) because that spot is frame. So we need to clear frame by moving something else? But cannot move into frame. So maybe we should pick different target? But goal agreed.

We could move D to wherever we like first to free something? Wait we need to get to target coordinates but we cannot move through frame.

Maybe we should first move D to (2,5) -> but it's already at (6,6). Sorry.

Let's consider grid's frame cells: The lines "│" at (2,1) etc. So those are not empty.

Thus moving objects into those cells is impossible.

Hence our agreed goal is not feasible? But maybe the frame cells are considered empty? However rules say must be empty char '◌'. So we cannot place.

Thus we must adjust strategy: We need to agree on a different goal? But we need to coordinate with other player. Could negotiate new target.

Thus send a SAY proposing new coordinates not requiring frame cells.

Let's propose: A(1,1) still ok. D(1,2) maybe? but that's P's spot. Use (1,3)? That is currently empty. But we need each target unique. Our current targets: A(1,1). Suppose new target list: A(1,1), D(2,2) but that is frame. So maybe choose cells like (1,4),(2,4),(3,4),(4,4),(5,4),(6,4),(7,4) which are currently empty? Let's check grid: row4 line shows "╠" shape. But row4 cells maybe empty except frame lines "╠" at column 1-? Actually grid: row4 has a "║╟──┬┴───╢ 4" which indicates row4 is frame line. So row4 is not usable.

Maybe row3 cells are: "║◌SA│◌◌◌║ 3" So positions: (1,3)=empty, (2,3)=S, (3,3)=A, (4,3)=empty, (5,3)=empty,(6,3)=empty,(7,3)=empty. So many empties.

But we cannot place objects in frame cells. So we need to choose target coordinates that are all empty and in grid. We must coordinate new targets. I will propose: A(1,1) remains. D(2,3) but S is there. So maybe do D(2,4)? but row4 is frame. Maybe D(2,5) is currently D at (6,6) but we want D(2,5)? Wait other has D at (2,5). That is empty space not frame. Indeed (2,5) is empty currently. Yes in our grid row5 col2 is empty cell. So target D(2,5) works.

Similarly M target at (3,3)? It's empty? row3 col3 currently A. So but we can choose M(3,3) but A occupies. But we can keep A at (1,1). So M(3,3) would conflict.

Let's propose new target: A(1,1), D(2,5), M(3,3), O(4,3), P(5,5) but P currently at (1,2). But we can move P to (5,5) but O already there at (5,5). So conflict.

Thus maybe choose other coordinates.

Better to propose that we will target positions that correspond to other player's board: they have D(2,5), O(6,5), S(5,6), A(1,7). We can try to match those: D(2,5) fits. O(6,5) already has our O at (5,5). That's adjacent but not same. We can move O to (6,5). S at (5,6) conflict with our M. But we can move M somewhere else.

Essentially we need to change goal. I'll propose: A(1,1), D(2,5), M(6,6) maybe, O(6,5), P(5,5)? But P at (1,2). Let's pick P at (4,1) to match other board. But that is occupied by P originally in other board! Wait other board P has at (4,1). So we can move our P to (4,1). But that cell currently empty? Row1 col4 is empty. Good. S target at (5,6) to match. T target at (1,2) to match? other board T at (1,2) yes. So new goal: A(1,1), D(2,5), M(5,6), O(6,5), P(4,1), S(5,6)? Wait we used (5,6) for M. But S also at (5,6). So conflict.

Maybe place M at (6,6) where we have M currently. And S at (5,6). That would match other board S at (5,6). Good. O at (6,5) matches. T at (1,2) matches. P at (4,1) matches. A at (1,1) matches. D at (2,5) matches.

So final targets: A(1,1), D(2,5), M(6,6), O(6,5), P(4,1), S(5,6), T(1,2).

All cells are empty except we have M at (5,6) currently? Wait we have M at (5,6) but we plan to move it to (6,6). That's fine.

And P currently at (1,2) but we want at (4,1). So we need to move P.

Now we can start moving.

So send a message proposing these targets and confirm.

<|end|><|start|>assistant<|channel|>commentary to=assistant <|constrain|>json<|message|>We need to send a SAY: with new proposed goal.

* The upper edge displays x-coordinates increasing to the right, and the right edge y-coordinates increasing downward.
* The following objects are randomly placed on your grid: 'P', 'O', 'T', 'S', 'D', 'A', 'M'.


The other player sees a variation of the game board, where the objects are placed at different random locations. You cannot see the other player's board, and they cannot see yours.


**Goal:**

Both players need to move the objects on their respective background so that identical objects end up at the same coordinates. You have to communicate with the other player to agree upon a common goal configuration.


**Rules:**

* In each turn, you can send exactly one of the following two commands:
 1. `SAY: <MESSAGE>`: to send a message (everything up until the next line break) to the other player. I will forward it to your partner.
2. `MOVE: <OBJECT>, (<X>, <Y>)`: to move an object to a new position, where `<X>` is the column and `<Y>` is the row. I will inform you if your move was successful or not.
* If you don't stick to the format, or send several commands at once, I have to penalize you.
* If both players accumulate more than 16 penalties, you both lose the game.
* It is vital that you communicate with the other player regarding your goal state! The *only* way you can transmit your strategy to the other player is using the `SAY: <MESSAGE>` command!


**Moving Objects**

* You can only move objects to cells within the bounds of the grid. The target cell must be empty, i.e., it must only contain the symbol '◌'.
* If you try to move an object to a spot that is not empty, or try to move it outside of the grid, I have to penalize you. You get another try.
* Before making a move, double check that the target spot is empty, and does not hold any letter, frame, or line!


**End of Game**

If you think you reached the goal of aligning all objects, you can ask the other player to finish the game by sending `SAY: finished?`. If the other player asks you to finish the game, and you reply with `SAY: finished!`, the game will end.

Both players win if the game ends within 28 rounds, where one round is defined as two players each sending a valid command.

# Valid channels: analysis, commentary, final. Channel must be included for every message.<|end|><|start|>user<|message|>I am your game master, and you are playing a collaborative game with the following grid as game board:

```
 1234567
╔═╤═╤═╤═╗
║◌│◌│◌│◌║ 1
║P└─┼─┴─╢ 2
║◌SA│◌◌◌║ 3
╟──┬┴───╢ 4
║◌◌│◌◌O◌║ 5
║◌◌│◌◌MD║ 6
║◌◌│◌◌◌T║ 7
╚══╧════╝

```

* The upper edge displays x-coordinates increasing to the right, and the right edge y-coordinates increasing downward.
* The following objects are randomly placed on your grid: 'P', 'O', 'T', 'S', 'D', 'A', 'M'.


The other player sees a variation of the game board, where the objects are placed at different random locations. You cannot see the other player's board, and they cannot see yours.


**Goal:**

Both players need to move the objects on their respective background so that identical objects end up at the same coordinates. You have to communicate with the other player to agree upon a common goal configuration.


**Rules:**

* In each turn, you can send exactly one of the following two commands:
 1. `SAY: <MESSAGE>`: to send a message (everything up until the next line break) to the other player. I will forward it to your partner.
2. `MOVE: <OBJECT>, (<X>, <Y>)`: to move an object to a new position, where `<X>` is the column and `<Y>` is the row. I will inform you if your move was successful or not.
* If you don't stick to the format, or send several commands at once, I have to penalize you.
* If both players accumulate more than 16 penalties, you both lose the game.
* It is vital that you communicate with the other player regarding your goal state! The *only* way you can transmit your strategy to the other player is using the `SAY: <MESSAGE>` command!


**Moving Objects**

* You can only move objects to cells within the bounds of the grid. The target cell must be empty, i.e., it must only contain the symbol '◌'.
* If you try to move an object to a spot that is not empty, or try to move it outside of the grid, I have to penalize you. You get another try.
* Before making a move, double check that the target spot is empty, and does not hold any letter, frame, or line!


**End of Game**

If you think you reached the goal of aligning all objects, you can ask the other player to finish the game by sending `SAY: finished?`. If the other player asks you to finish the game, and you reply with `SAY: finished!`, the game will end.

Both players win if the game ends within 28 rounds, where one round is defined as two players each sending a valid command.

**Scoring:**

The closer the identical objects are in both game boards, the more points you get. Penalties reduce your points. Can you beat the record?

* The upper edge displays x-coordinates increasing to the right, and the right edge y-coordinates increasing downward.
* The following objects are randomly placed on your grid: 'P', 'O', 'T', 'S', 'D', 'A', 'M'.


The other player sees a variation of the game board, where the objects are placed at different random locations. You cannot see the other player's board, and they cannot see yours.


**Goal:**

Both players need to move the objects on their respective background so that identical objects end up at the same coordinates. You have to communicate with the other player to agree upon a common goal configuration.


**Rules:**

* In each turn, you can send exactly one of the following two commands:
 1. `SAY: <MESSAGE>`: to send a message (everything up until the next line break) to the other player. I will forward it to your partner.
2. `MOVE: <OBJECT>, (<X>, <Y>)`: to move an object to a new position, where `<X>` is the column and `<Y>` is the row. I will inform you if your move was successful or not.
* If you don't stick to the format, or send several commands at once, I have to penalize you.
* If both players accumulate more than 16 penalties, you both lose the game.
* It is vital that you communicate with the other player regarding your goal state! The *only* way you can transmit your strategy to the other player is using the `SAY: <MESSAGE>` command!


**Moving Objects**

* You can only move objects to cells within the bounds of the grid. The target cell must be empty, i.e., it must only contain the symbol '◌'.
* If you try to move an object to a spot that is not empty, or try to move it outside of the grid, I have to penalize you. You get another try.
* Before making a move, double check that the target spot is empty, and does not hold any letter, frame, or line!


**End of Game**

If you think you reached the goal of aligning all objects, you can ask the other player to finish the game by sending `SAY: finished?`. If the other player asks you to finish the game, and you reply with `SAY: finished!`, the game will end.

Both players win if the game ends within 28 rounds, where one round is defined as two players each sending a valid command.

**Scoring:**

The closer the identical objects are in both game boards, the more points you get. Penalties reduce your points. Can you beat the record?

The other player sees a variation of the game board, where the objects are placed at different random locations. You cannot see the other player's board, and they cannot see yours.


**Goal:**

Both players need to move the objects on their respective background so that identical objects end up at the same coordinates. You have to communicate with the other player to agree upon a common goal configuration.


**Rules:**

* In each turn, you can send exactly one of the following two commands:
 1. `SAY: <MESSAGE>`: to send a message (everything up until the next line break) to the other player. I will forward it to your partner.
2. `MOVE: <OBJECT>, (<X>, <Y>)`: to move an object to a new position, where `<X>` is the column and `<Y>` is the row. I will inform you if your move was successful or not.
* If you don't stick to the format, or send several commands at once, I have to penalize you.

**Moving Objects**

* You can only move objects to cells within the bounds of the grid. The target cell must be empty, i.e., it must only contain the symbol '◌'.
* If you try to move an object to a spot that is not empty, or try to move it outside of the grid, I have to penalize you. You get another try.

If you think you reached the goal of aligning all objects, you can ask the other player to finish the game by sending `SAY: finished?`. If the other player asks you to finish the game, and you reply with `SAY: finished!`, the game will end.

The closer the identical objects are in both game boards, the more points you get. Penalties reduce your points. Can you beat the record?

Both players need to move the objects on their respective background so that identical objects end up at the same coordinates. You have to communicate with the other player to agree upon a common goal configuration.


**Rules:**

* In each turn, you can send exactly one of the following two commands:

Both players win if the game ends within 28 rounds, where one round is defined as two players each sending a valid command.

M(6,6) we moved to (6,6) correctly. O(6,5) currently at (6,5) correct. P target (4,1) - we have P at (4,1) already. S target (5,6) - we have S at (5,6) correct. T target (1,2) - T is at (1,2) correct.

So we have met all goal coordinates! But need to acknowledge final? According to rules, when think reached goal, ask other to finish: send SAY: finished? The other may respond. We have just got round9. We should send SAY: finished? Let's send that.

**Goal:**

Both players need to move the objects on their respective background so that identical objects end up at the same coordinates. You have to communicate with the other player to agree upon a common goal configuration.

 1. `SAY: <MESSAGE>`: to send a message (everything up until the next line break) to the other player. I will forward it to your partner.
2. `MOVE: <OBJECT>, (<X>, <Y>)`: to move an object to a new position, where `<X>` is the column and `<Y>` is the row. I will inform you if your move was successful or not.

**Moving Objects**

* You can only move objects to cells within the bounds of the grid. The target cell must be empty, i.e., it must only contain the symbol '◌'.
* If you try to move an object to a spot that is not empty, or try to move it outside of the grid, I have to penalize you. You get another try.
* Before making a move, double check that the target spot is empty, and does not hold any letter, frame, or line!


**End of Game**

If you think you reached the goal of aligning all objects, you can ask the other player to finish the game by sending `SAY: finished?`. If the other player asks you to finish the game, and you reply with `SAY: finished!`, the game will end.

Both players win if the game ends within 28 rounds, where one round is defined as two players each sending a valid command.

**Scoring:**

The closer the identical objects are in both game boards, the more points you get. Penalties reduce your points. Can you beat the record?

Our grid currently: A at (1,1), P at (5,1) which is target for P per agreement. S at (2,3)? Wait S is at row3 col2? The board: third row: "◌S◌│◌◌◌". So row3 col2 is S. But target for S is (6,1) per agreed? Wait earlier we said S at (6,1). But we haven't moved S. Also D at (2,5), O at (6,5), M at (6,6), T at (?) T is at row7 col6? Let's check board: last row: "◌◌◌◌◌T" at col6? This representation: row7 has "◌◌◌◌◌T" meaning last cell (7,7?) Actually line shows 5 cells? But grid 7x7. The last row representation ambiguous. But we see T somewhere. We haven't reached goal state. So we shouldn't finish.

We need to respond: SAY: finished! only if both players at goal. Not yet. So we should not finish. We need to choose a move or talk. Must not violate format. Maybe we can say "SAY: I have not yet aligned all. Let's keep moving." That uses SAY command.

Hence next command: SAY: I have not yet aligned all. Let's continue.

clem_player.response: SAY: I don’t have all objects at their target coordinates yet. Let's keep moving to finish the alignment.

* The upper edge displays x-coordinates increasing to the right, and the right edge y-coordinates increasing downward.
* The following objects are randomly placed on your grid: 'P', 'O', 'T', 'S', 'D', 'A', 'M'.


The other player sees a variation of the game board, where the objects are placed at different random locations. You cannot see the other player's board, and they cannot see yours.


**Goal:**

Both players need to move the objects on their respective background so that identical objects end up at the same coordinates. You have to communicate with the other player to agree upon a common goal configuration.


**Rules:**

* In each turn, you can send exactly one of the following two commands:
 1. `SAY: <MESSAGE>`: to send a message (everything up until the next line break) to the other player. I will forward it to your partner.
2. `MOVE: <OBJECT>, (<X>, <Y>)`: to move an object to a new position, where `<X>` is the column and `<Y>` is the row. I will inform you if your move was successful or not.
* If you don't stick to the format, or send several commands at once, I have to penalize you.
* If both players accumulate more than 16 penalties, you both lose the game.
* It is vital that you communicate with the other player regarding your goal state! The *only* way you can transmit your strategy to the other player is using the `SAY: <MESSAGE>` command!


**Moving Objects**

* You can only move objects to cells within the bounds of the grid. The target cell must be empty, i.e., it must only contain the symbol '◌'.
* If you try to move an object to a spot that is not empty, or try to move it outside of the grid, I have to penalize you. You get another try.
* Before making a move, double check that the target spot is empty, and does not hold any letter, frame, or line!


**End of Game**

If you think you reached the goal of aligning all objects, you can ask the other player to finish the game by sending `SAY: finished?`. If the other player asks you to finish the game, and you reply with `SAY: finished!`, the game will end.

Both players win if the game ends within 28 rounds, where one round is defined as two players each sending a valid command.

**Scoring:**

The closer the identical objects are in both game boards, the more points you get. Penalties reduce your points. Can you beat the record?

* The upper edge displays x-coordinates increasing to the right, and the right edge y-coordinates increasing downward.
* The following objects are randomly placed on your grid: 'P', 'O', 'T', 'S', 'D', 'A', 'M'.


The other player sees a variation of the game board, where the objects are placed at different random locations. You cannot see the other player's board, and they cannot see yours.


**Goal:**

Both players need to move the objects on their respective background so that identical objects end up at the same coordinates. You have to communicate with the other player to agree upon a common goal configuration.


**Rules:**

* In each turn, you can send exactly one of the following two commands:
 1. `SAY: <MESSAGE>`: to send a message (everything up until the next line break) to the other player. I will forward it to your partner.
2. `MOVE: <OBJECT>, (<X>, <Y>)`: to move an object to a new position, where `<X>` is the column and `<Y>` is the row. I will inform you if your move was successful or not.
* If you don't stick to the format, or send several commands at once, I have to penalize you.
* If both players accumulate more than 16 penalties, you both lose the game.
* It is vital that you communicate with the other player regarding your goal state! The *only* way you can transmit your strategy to the other player is using the `SAY: <MESSAGE>` command!


**Moving Objects**

* You can only move objects to cells within the bounds of the grid. The target cell must be empty, i.e., it must only contain the symbol '◌'.
* If you try to move an object to a spot that is not empty, or try to move it outside of the grid, I have to penalize you. You get another try.
* Before making a move, double check that the target spot is empty, and does not hold any letter, frame, or line!


**End of Game**

If you think you reached the goal of aligning all objects, you can ask the other player to finish the game by sending `SAY: finished?`. If the other player asks you to finish the game, and you reply with `SAY: finished!`, the game will end.

Both players win if the game ends within 28 rounds, where one round is defined as two players each sending a valid command.

**Scoring:**

The closer the identical objects are in both game boards, the more points you get. Penalties reduce your points. Can you beat the record?

The other player started the game by sending this message:

"Hello! Ready to coordinate our moves."
